headline: Defence Budget Stagnates at 1.1% of GDP for 5 Years
context: Malaysia 2026 defence budget of RM18.2 billion represents 1.1% of GDP unchanged for 5 years and below NATO 2% standard.

cards.big: Defence spending stagnant at 1.1% of GDP for 5 consecutive years
cards.sub: Malaysia underspends compared to regional neighbours and benchmarks

cards.big: Singapore spends 3.2% of GDP with budget 4 times Malaysia
cards.sub: Indonesia and Thailand spend 1.4% and 1.5% respectively

cards.big: 48% of RMAF fighter aircraft grounded due to maintenance issues
cards.sub: Fleet of 28 Su-30MKM and 8 FA-18D has only 18 operational at any time

cards.big: Military housing in 35 camps classified as substandard
cards.sub: 2,800 experienced personnel left in 2025 citing poor conditions

cards.big: Stagnant budgets test whether defence readiness is a stated or funded priority
1.1% of GDP on defence for 5 straight years. Strategic ambiguity depends on capability, which requires spending.
